title: Agent retrieval index manifest
description: Build-time, committed chunk manifest that powers `pnpm agent:find`. Vectors live out of git in a content-hash cache (Phase 2); this manifest is the queryable, diffable source of truth. See docs/04-governance/adr/0066-agent-semantic-retrieval-over-knowledge-graph.md.

sourceHashes.description: SHA-256 of each input source, so a stale manifest is detectable without re-reading every file.

id.description: Stable chunk id: `<type>:<slug>` for graph nodes, `export:<package>:<name>` for symbols.

path.description: Repo-relative pointer the agent should open.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the source artifact (e.g. Active / Deprecated), when known — lets agents discount stale sources.

text.description: Normalized searchable text: title + headings + key terms. Used for lexical ranking and Phase-2 embedding.

contentHash.description: SHA-256 of `text` — Phase-2 vector cache key, also drives incremental re-embedding.
